name: Spanish Cobras
description: The Insane Spanish Cobras are a primarily Latino (Puerto Rican/Mexican) Folk Nation street gang founded circa 1958 in Bridgeport, Chicago as 'Loco De Culebro' (Crazy Snakes). Richard 'King Cobra' Medina created the 'Insane' concepts in the mid-1970s, adopting black and green colors. Medina was assassinated by Insane Unknowns in 1979 at age 20. In 1989, Cobras, Maniac Latin Disciples, and Two Sixers formed Spanish Growth and Development (SGD), modeled after the Chicago Outfit, to control shootings — it eventually collapsed. The Insane-Maniac war escalated in 1996 making Chicago Tribune headlines. Led by Anibal 'Tuffy C' Santiago. Present in Chicago (North/West sides), Milwaukee, Racine, Kenosha, Detroit, Ohio, Connecticut, and Florida.
